title: 10 ATM and POS Settlement Process
body_text:
10	ATM and POS Settlement Process
10.1	ATM and POS Settlement
Central 1 posts daily net settlement amounts for ATM and POS transactions to your financial institutionâs Central 1 current account. US dollar transactions are converted to Canadian dollars and included in the net settlement.
The table below displays codes applied to ATM and POS settlement amounts.
Type
ATM
POS
Settlement
TM
ES
Adjustment
TA
EA
Everlink Payment Services Inc. and Cardtronics provide Central 1 with daily settlement posting files for ATM and POS transactions. The files are delivered to Central 1 the evening of the date of the transactions. Central 1 posts the appropriate entries to your financial institutionâs current account with Central 1. On weekends or statutory holidays, the entries are posted the next business day.
Refer to the table below for settlement cut-off times for ATM and POS transactions.

10.2	ATM and POS Settlement Differences
Reconcile the settlement totals posted to your financial institutionâs Central 1 current account with your banking systemâs transaction log.
Youâre responsible for ensuring that the ATM and POS settlement totals processed by the C2C Node or Cooperative Node, as applicable, agree with the settlement totals provided by your financial institutionâs banking system.
If the amount on the ATM and POS network report doesnât match the amount posted to your financial institutionâs Central 1 account, follow the applicable procedures below:
Financial institutions on the C2C Node
â Contact Cardtronics directly.
Financial institutions on the Cooperative Node
â Email the details to
epay@central1.com.
Central 1 will make an adjustment if there is an error in the entry posted to your financial institutionâs current account.
10.3	ATM and POS Report Distribution
Credit unions under the C2C Node receive their daily settlement and adjustment reports from Cardtronics. Credit unions under the Cooperative Node receive their daily settlement and adjustment reports from Everlink. Central 1 doesnât send out reports.
Important
:	ATM and POS transaction records (paper or electronic) must be retained for seven years.
END
